weather_report:
Morning in Osborne Park arrives under a polished blue, the sort of clear sky that sharpens edges and sets windows gleaming. The forecast keeps it simple and kind: sun now, sun at midday, sun into early afternoon, with barely a hint of cloud to smudge the scene. Expect a bright, glare-prone day with excellent visibility; UV will rise toward midday into the kind that nips at unguarded skin even in autumn, then ease later.

It looks pleasantly workable rather than sweltering: warm in the sun, a touch cooler in the shade. Light layers and breathable fabrics will serve you well; add sunglasses, a brimmed hat, and a dab of sunscreen if you’re out around lunch. It’s a good day for mundane victories—hang a load of washing, walk a small errand, tend a few plants, or claim a shaded table for a quiet coffee. If you fancy a stretch of the legs around noon, keep to the tree-lined side of the street and let the clear light do the talking.
